findings.title: CVE-2026-34621 — Adobe Acrobat Reader Prototype Pollution Zero-Day Actively Exploited Since December 2025
findings.summary: Prototype pollution in Adobe Acrobat/Reader allows malicious PDFs to bypass sandbox restrictions and execute arbitrary code via privileged JavaScript APIs, actively exploited since December 2025 with Russian oil/gas themed lures, patched April 13.

findings.title: CVE-2026-39987 — Marimo Python Notebook Pre-Auth RCE via Unauthenticated WebSocket Terminal
findings.summary: Unauthenticated WebSocket terminal endpoint in Marimo grants full shell access, actively exploited within 10 hours of disclosure for credential theft across 125+ attacking IPs.

findings.title: CVE-2026-5194 — wolfSSL ECDSA Signature Verification Bypass Enables Certificate Forgery Across 5 Billion Devices
findings.summary: Missing hash digest size and OID checks in wolfSSL ECDSA verification allow forged TLS certificates to be accepted as genuine, affecting an estimated 5 billion IoT and embedded devices worldwide.

findings.title: Juniper Junos OS — Critical Unauthenticated Device Takeover Patched Among Dozens of Vulnerabilities
findings.summary: Juniper patched dozens of Junos OS vulnerabilities including a critical flaw exploitable remotely without authentication to fully take over network devices.

findings.title: CVE-2026-1731 — BeyondTrust Remote Support Command Injection via WebSocket with Public PoC
findings.affected_technology: BeyondTrust Remote Support / Privileged Remote Access
findings.summary: Command injection via unsafe Bash arithmetic in a WebSocket-reachable script in BeyondTrust remote access products, with a public PoC exploit gaining traction on GitHub.

findings.title: CPUID Supply Chain Attack — Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Deliver STX RAT
findings.summary: Attackers compromised CPUID's API and redirected official download links to serve trojanized executables containing STX RAT for approximately 19 hours on April 9-10.

findings.title: PraisonAI Agent Framework — Three Critical Vulnerabilities Enable Unauthenticated RCE and Session Hijacking
findings.summary: Three critical vulnerabilities in the PraisonAI AI agent framework allow unauthenticated WebSocket session hijacking, arbitrary code execution via YAML, and code injection via unsanitized imports.

findings.title: Chrome 147 Patches 60 Vulnerabilities Including Two Critical WebML Flaws
findings.summary: Google released Chrome 147 with fixes for 60 security vulnerabilities including two critical flaws in the WebML component earning $86,000 in combined bounties.

noted.summary: Unauthenticated RCE via JMX monitoring port (CVSS 9.8); enterprise ETL platform but JMX ports typically not internet-facing.
Rapid7 published incident response findings confirming active exploitation; limited technical details available.
Multiple heap buffer overflows enabling RCE (CERT/CC VU#536588); niche medical imaging server relevant to healthcare orgs.
Remote command execution in Frigate <= 0.16.3 with public PoC on GitHub; IoT/home automation NVR with limited enterprise exposure.
Stored XSS in versions 1.98.0-1.130.9; limited direct impact but notable for AI platform security tracking.
PoC exploit with 21 GitHub stars published; insufficient details to classify severity. Monitoring.
